assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable already recognised.
The relevant analysis is limited to identifying the applicable top-level hedge accounting models, not concluding eligibility.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable can raise foreign exchange risk on a recognised monetary item, which is one of the core IFRS hedge accounting models to assess.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS identifies cash flow hedging as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model, including for eligible forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS separately addresses hedges of foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation in consolidated financial statements.
